No Poverty.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to poverty eradication. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to hunger eradication or food security. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to health or well-being outcomes. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education (Target 4.3) providing advanced technical skills for employment (Target 4.4). Core competence D, 'understand and identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' aligns with Target 4.7.

Gender Equality.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to gender equality. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to water or sanitation. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to energy. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops advanced programming and financial modeling skills, contributing to technological upgrading (Target 8.2) and strengthening financial institutions (Target 8.10). Core competence D supports responsible economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course focuses on advanced C++ programming and QuantLib for complex financial applications, directly enhancing technological capabilities and fostering innovation in the financial industry (Target 9.5, 9.b).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course teaches '風險計量模型' (risk measurement models), essential for improving financial market regulation (Target 10.5). Core competence D reinforces the importance of equitable financial systems.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to sustainable cities or communities. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to responsible consumption or production. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Climate Action.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to climate action. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to marine life. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Life on Land.reason: No direct evidence links the course content to terrestrial ecosystems. A minimal score is assigned for general academic contribution.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Skills in building '風險計量模型' (risk measurement models) can indirectly contribute to more effective, accountable, and transparent financial institutions (Target 16.6). Core competence D supports responsible institutions.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course uses the open-source 'QuantLib' library, promoting knowledge sharing (Target 17.6), and develops risk modeling skills, contributing to macroeconomic stability (Target 17.13). Core competence D aligns with sustainable development.
